title: Living and Exercising in Space on the ISS
passage:
Living on the ISS

Hello, everyone. My name is Jessica Meir. I am an astronaut. I work on the International Space Station (国际空间站,缩写为ISS) as a flight engineer. Today, I will tell you something about what life is like on the ISS.

The ISS has over 425 cubic meters(立方米) of living space. I work here with astronauts from the United States, Russia, Japan, Canada and several other European countries. When we are on the ISS, we have to follow a detailed daily schedule. It tells us when to get up, eat, exercise, work, relax and go to sleep. We spend about two hours each day exercising. Without regular exercise, we would lose a certain amount of muscle and bone due to the microgravity(微重力) environment in space.

When we are not busy doing experiments on the ISS, we can relax and have fun through a variety of ways. Like most of you, we listen to music, read books, play cards, watch movies and talk with our family members. As you can see, the microgravity environment in space brings many challenges that don't exist on the Earth. Would you like to live for some time on the ISS?

Yours sincerely,
Jessica Meir

questions.prompt: Where are the astronauts on the ISS from?
options: The United States, Russia, Japan, Canada and several other European countries.
Only the United States and Russia.

questions.prompt: How long do the astronauts exercise every day?
How do Jessica and other astronauts relax and have fun on the ISS?
options: By listening to music, reading books, playing cards and watching movies.

questions.prompt: What do you think of the life on the ISS? Why? Write 20 words or more.

blocks.text: I think life on the ISS is extremely challenging but exciting.
Because astronauts have to live in a microgravity environment and exercise two hours daily to stay strong.
It would be an amazing experience to do experiments and see the Earth from space.
I think living in space is quite boring and lonely.
Because they cannot eat fresh food or take a normal shower.
Overall, it is a difficult journey that I would never want to try.
